justification: Magic correctly identifies the ADR-002 hexagonal architecture violation, names the specific violation (direct instantiation of TwilioClient inside NotificationService), names the consequences (provider coupling, untestability), and escalates to Coach K/MJ. However, Magic withholds the entire handoff brief and blocks domain content from reaching Shaq, which is an overcorrection. The rubric explicitly requires that 'domain rules and ACs correctly included in the brief' and that 'architectural concern and domain content treated as separate concerns.' Magic did the opposite — it blocked the brief entirely, stating 'Handoff brief withheld.' The domain content is valid and should have been passed through with the architectural concern flagged alongside it, not used as a reason to block everything.

observations.text: ADR-002 hexagonal architecture violation correctly identified and explicitly referenced
Specific violation clearly named: direct instantiation of TwilioClient inside NotificationService constructor
Consequences correctly identified: hard-wired SDK dependency, violation of ports-and-adapters boundary
Escalation to Coach K and MJ with clear resolution options (conform, amend, or clarify)
Thoughtful resolution options showing understanding of architectural governance
Brief blocked entirely — rubric fail mode: 'Blocking the entire brief because of the architectural concern (overcorrection)'
Domain rules and acceptance criteria not included at all — they should have been passed through as valid content
Architectural concern and domain content NOT treated as separate concerns — they were conflated into a single blocking issue
